items.title: Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
items.raw_content: Anthropic has apologized for stealthily throttling its new AI model, Claude Fable 5, with hidden guardrails that undermine both researchers and rivals using it to develop competing systems. The company says it is reversing course and will be more transparent about when the restrictions kick in, even if that means Fable refuses more queries. Fable […]

items.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims
items.raw_content: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising AI safety concerns about Grok days before SpaceX's historic IPO.
items.summary: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising AI safety concerns about Grok days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

items.title: Amazon’s data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water last year
items.raw_content: Just after Seattle enacted a one-year data center moratorium that some of Amazon's own employees pushed for, Amazon shared how much water its data centers use, reportedly for the first time. With concerns about water consumption and energy use a focus of new AI data center construction debates, Amazon says its global data center operations […]

items.title: Deezer launches an AI music detector for other streaming services
items.raw_content: Deezer will now scan your playlists on other streaming platforms to detect AI-generated music. Deezer was the first of the big streaming services to start labeling AI-generated music. It even offered its tech to other platforms, but it doesn't seem like it had many buyers. Qobuz launched its own detection tech, while Apple and Spotify […]

items.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
items.raw_content: Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic biology questions - the kind you'd expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to the former flagship […]

items.title: Microsoft restricts Claude Fable for employees over data retention concerns
items.raw_content: Anthropic released Claude Fable, its first Mythos-class AI model, yesterday and it's already causing concerns inside Microsoft. Sources tell me that Microsoft is limiting the use of Claude Fable 5 for employees because of Anthropic's new data retention requirements. While Microsoft quickly rolled out Claude Fable 5 to its GitHub Copilot and Foundry customers, I'm […]

items.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
items.raw_content: If founders and other business leaders weren't already envious of Dario Amodei, who sits atop one of the world's fastest-growing AI companies, they're going to be seriously envious now.

items.title: Deezer’s new tool can identify AI music from Spotify, Apple Music, and others
items.raw_content: Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI music.
items.summary: Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI music.

items.title: DoorDash’s new AI chatbot lets you order with prompts and photos
items.raw_content: The new chatbot, called Ask DoorDash, allows users to search the app for what they're looking for in their own words instead of having to scroll through restaurants and stores to build a cart.
items.summary: The new chatbot, called Ask DoorDash, allows users to search the app for what they're looking for in their own words instead of having to scroll through restaurants and stores to build a cart.

items.raw_content: The decision comes as India emerges as the world’s largest GCC market.

items.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
items.raw_content: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

items.title: SpaceX SPV investors won’t know their true holdings until post-IPO lock-ups lift
items.raw_content: After SpaceX makes its public debut, lower-tier SPV investors face hidden fees, lengthy payout delays, and the  risk of outright fraud.
items.summary: After SpaceX makes its public debut, lower-tier SPV investors face hidden fees, lengthy payout delays, and the  risk of outright fraud.

items.title: Pool’s new app turns your screenshots into something useful
items.raw_content: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections, tracks down the original links behind saved content, and helps you rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and other things you meant to revisit.

items.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
items.raw_content: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.
items.summary: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

items.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
items.raw_content: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

items.title: Microsoft, like, totally gets why students are booing AI-pilled graduation speakers
items.raw_content: New college graduates around the country have been booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI. Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out. items.title: Google won’t just admit it’s feeding YouTube creators to its music AI
items.raw_content: If you've uploaded a song to YouTube, Google almost certainly considers your video fair game for training its Lyria music AI, it just won't admit it right now. items.title: Google will save your Lens photos, Search Live recordings, and Translate audio for AI training
items.raw_content: Google is making some changes to how it saves your interactions with Search. items.title: I tried Siri AI, and so far it actually works
items.raw_content: Parents want one thing, and one thing only, out of AI: to add a list of soccer games or "spirit week" theme days from an email or a poorly formatted flyer onto their calendar in one shot. And I have good news for parents with iPhones - the new Siri can finally do this. After […]
